100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 14, 1926, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin announced a new plan to address the country’s ongoing economic challenges, aimed at stabilizing the post-war recovery. In the United States, a major storm swept through the Midwest, causing significant damage and prompting emergency responses in several states. Meanwhile, the League of Nations continued discussions on disarmament, reflecting ongoing global efforts to prevent future conflicts.
